title: PII redaction strategies
description: PII can be removed, masked, or reversibly tokenized; reversible tokenization preserves utility but needs a protected mapping.
body: Use when deciding how to handle personal data in prompts. Removal drops the data entirely (simplest, lowest utility); masking replaces it with a type placeholder (keeps shape, irreversible); tokenization swaps it for a stable token with a mapping kept outside the prompt so post-processing can re-hydrate (highest utility, requires protecting the map). Do not send raw PII to the model, and avoid storing the token map alongside the prompt. Actions: choose by whether downstream needs the real value and protect any mapping. Validation signals include a test that seeded PII never appears in the outbound request under each strategy. Risk boundary: detection is imperfect, so fail closed on uncertainty and defer the policy to risk.privacy.
